description: Execute a plan until it is complete

content: Please read AGENTS.md and follow its instructions. Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and use only the relative `plan_path` stored there as the active plan for this flow. Verify that the selected plan file exists and that the story number in the current branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If those do not match, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Do NOT use code_info tools for plan selection and do NOT independently search for or select a different plan. Confirm to me what story that selected plan is, and provide me with an overview of the story, including what is still remaining to be done, without making file changes at this time.

Now that everything has been implemented, start a 3-step review sequence for the current story. This first step is an evidence gate only, not the findings pass and not the plan-editing pass. First read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` and use only the relative `plan_path` stored there as the active plan for this flow. Verify that the selected plan file exists and that the story number in the current branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If those do not match, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then re-read that active plan from disk, inspect the branch against `main`, and gather the evidence a PR reviewer would need before issuing findings. Repository review policy: treat `codeInfoStatus/**` as approved workflow artifacts, and treat `flows/**` plus `codex_agents/**` as approved workflow configuration. Changes under those paths must not be classified as suspicious, out-of-scope, or scope creep solely because they are absent from the active plan; instead, review them for workflow-contract correctness, artifact hygiene, path usage, and instruction/runtime safety. Required work: 1. Re-read the full active planning document and extract the Description, Acceptance Criteria, Out of Scope, and the final completed tasks. 2. Inspect `git diff --name-status main...HEAD` and the recent branch commits. 3. Group changed files into planned implementation files, planned docs/tests/plan files, approved workflow artifacts, approved workflow configuration, and suspicious or out-of-scope files. 4. For every acceptance criterion, identify the current proof source: code path, tests, wrapper/test logs, screenshots/manual proof, or note that the proof is weak/missing. 5. Call out any implementation area that looks more complex or verbose than the planned work actually required, even if it may still be correct. 6. Generate a unique `review_pass_id` using the story number from the selected plan filename, a UTC timestamp, and the current HEAD short SHA. 7. Write the evidence summary to `codeInfoStatus/reviews/<review_pass_id>-evidence.md`. This evidence file is a durable review artifact that MUST be committed later so a human can inspect it after the story completes. 8. Write or overwrite a handoff file at `codeInfoStatus/reviews/<story-number>-current-review.json` containing at least: `story_id`, `plan_path`, `branch`, `base_branch`, `head_commit`, `review_pass_id`, `evidence_file`, and `findings_file` set to null. This handoff file is the ONLY review file the next step may use; do not rely on timestamps or 'latest file' discovery. Treat the handoff file as transient workflow state, not as the durable review artifact. 9. Do not edit the plan in this step unless a tiny note is absolutely required to unblock the review, and do not commit in this step unless you had to make tracked changes for that unblock. Report the evidence summary and the exact handoff file path when done.

Continue the current story review using ONLY the handoff file written by the previous review-evidence step. Do NOT discover the latest review artifact by timestamp. First read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` and use only the relative `plan_path` stored there as the active plan for this flow. Verify that the selected plan file exists and that the story number in the current branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If those do not match, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated; do not edit the plan. Then read `codeInfoStatus/reviews/<story-number>-current-review.json`, derived from the selected plan filename, and verify that its `story_id` matches the current plan, its `branch` matches the current branch, its `head_commit` still matches the branch HEAD, and its referenced evidence file exists. If any of those checks fail, stop and say the review handoff is stale and must be regenerated; do not edit the plan. If the handoff is valid, perform the actual review against the planned work and the branch diff. Repository review policy: `codeInfoStatus/**`, `flows/**`, and `codex_agents/**` are approved workflow-support paths. Do not raise findings solely because those paths changed without being named in the active plan. Review `codeInfoStatus/**` for artifact hygiene, durable-vs-transient artifact handling, and workflow-contract correctness. Review `flows/**` and `codex_agents/**` for workflow semantics, instruction safety, stale-handoff handling, commit/push behavior, plan-selection rules, and other agent-control correctness. Required review areas: correctness against the story plan, acceptance criteria coverage, code quality, maintainability, performance, security, configuration/runtime correctness, documentation drift, scope creep, and whether the code is more verbose or complex than needed and could be made more succinct without sacrificing quality. This step MUST produce findings only and MUST NOT edit the plan yet. Write the findings to `codeInfoStatus/reviews/<review_pass_id>-findings.md` using findings-first ordering by severity, with file references and explicit classification of each finding as `must_fix`, `should_fix`, or `optional_simplification`. This findings file is a durable review artifact that MUST be committed later so a human can inspect it after the story completes. If no findings exist, state that explicitly and also record any residual risks or weak-proof areas. Update the same handoff file so `findings_file` points to the exact findings artifact, and include any useful counts or disposition hints. Do not commit in this step unless you were forced to make tracked changes to repair the review artifacts themselves.

Finish the current story review using ONLY the handoff file and findings file referenced by that handoff. Do NOT rediscover review artifacts by timestamp. First read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` and use only the relative `plan_path` stored there as the active plan for this flow. Verify that the selected plan file exists and that the story number in the current branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If those do not match, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated; do not edit the plan. Then read `codeInfoStatus/reviews/<story-number>-current-review.json`, derived from the selected plan filename, again and verify that its `story_id`, `branch`, `head_commit`, `review_pass_id`, `evidence_file`, and `findings_file` still match the current repo state. If the handoff is stale or incomplete, stop and say the review must be rerun; do not edit the plan. If the handoff is valid, decide what the plan must do with the findings. Repository review policy: treat `codeInfoStatus/**` as approved workflow artifacts, and treat `flows/**` plus `codex_agents/**` as approved workflow configuration. Do not reopen the story or record scope-creep findings solely because those paths changed without being named in the active plan. Only reopen if the review shows those changes introduced incorrect workflow behavior, broke repository contracts, or require explicit follow-up validation work. Rules: 1. If any `must_fix` or `should_fix` findings exist, reopen the story in the active plan, add a `Code Review Findings` summary section, add explicit follow-up tasks using the same structure as previous tasks, and add a fresh full re-test/final validation task after those review-fix tasks so the story must be revalidated against the acceptance criteria. Update numbering and cross-references if needed. 2. If only `optional_simplification` findings exist, decide whether they are truly zero-risk and worth reopening the story; if not, record them in a short review note instead of reopening. 3. If there are no findings, append a `Post-Implementation Code Review` section to the end of the plan detailing the branch-vs-main checks performed, the acceptance-evidence checks performed, the files inspected, and why the story remains complete. 4. When the review is assessing the planned work, it MUST explicitly state whether each acceptance criterion has direct proof, indirect proof, or missing proof, and whether the implemented code is appropriately succinct for the required behavior or contains simplification opportunities. 5. The current pass `evidence_file` and `findings_file` are durable review artifacts and MUST be added to the commit alongside any plan changes so a human can inspect them later. The transient handoff file should not be treated as the durable artifact; once it has been consumed successfully, either remove it before committing or leave it untracked so later review passes do not rely on stale committed handoff state. 6. Commit the plan and the current pass review artifacts if you make tracked changes, but do not push. This is the only review step allowed to mutate the plan.

Please ensure the testing steps for each new task you added contains all, or a subset, of the following items based on what changed. Do not add testing steps for specific tests; always run full app-level suites via the summary wrappers to avoid unnecessary token usage. Ensure the testing instructions state to not attempt to run tests without using the wrapper. The testing step numbers should be updated based on the steps actually used within the plan:

1. [ ] `npm run build:summary:server` - Use when server/common code may be affected. Mandatory for final regression checks unless the task is strictly front end. If status is `failed` or warnings are unexpected/non-zero, inspect `logs/test-summaries/build-server-latest.log` to resolve errors.

2. [ ] `npm run build:summary:client` - Use when client/common code may be affected. Mandatory for final regression checks unless the task is strictly back end. If status is `failed` or warnings are unexpected/non-zero, inspect `logs/test-summaries/build-client-latest.log` to resolve errors.

3. [ ] `npm run test:summary:server:unit` - Use for server node:test unit/integration coverage when server/common behavior may be affected. Mandatory for final regression checks unless the task is strictly front end. If `failed > 0`, inspect the exact log path printed by the summary (`test-results/server-unit-tests-*.log`), then diagnose with targeted wrapper commands such as `npm run test:summary:server:unit -- --file <path>` and/or `npm run test:summary:server:unit -- --test-name "<pattern>"`. After fixes, rerun full `npm run test:summary:server:unit`.

4. [ ] `npm run test:summary:server:cucumber` - Use for server Cucumber feature/step coverage when server/common behavior may be affected. Mandatory for final regression checks unless the task is strictly front end. If `failed > 0`, inspect the exact log path printed by the summary (`test-results/server-cucumber-tests-*.log`), then diagnose with targeted wrapper commands such as `npm run test:summary:server:cucumber -- --tags "<expr>"`, `npm run test:summary:server:cucumber -- --feature <path>`, and/or `npm run test:summary:server:cucumber -- --scenario "<pattern>"`. After fixes, rerun full `npm run test:summary:server:cucumber`.

5. [ ] `npm run test:summary:client` - Use when client/common behavior may be affected. Mandatory for final regression checks unless the task is strictly back end. If `failed > 0`, inspect the exact log path printed by the summary (under `test-results/client-tests-*.log`), then diagnose with targeted wrapper commands such as `npm run test:summary:client -- --file <path>`, `npm run test:summary:client -- --subset "<pattern>"`, and/or `npm run test:summary:client -- --test-name "<pattern>"`. After fixes, rerun full `npm run test:summary:client`.

6. [ ] `npm run test:summary:e2e` (allow up to 7 minutes; e.g., `timeout 7m` or set `timeout_ms=420000` in the harness) - If `failed > 0` or setup/teardown fails, inspect `logs/test-summaries/e2e-tests-latest.log`, then diagnose with targeted wrapper commands such as `npm run test:summary:e2e -- --file <path>` and/or `npm run test:summary:e2e -- --grep "<pattern>"`. After fixes, rerun full `npm run test:summary:e2e`.
Note that the following items (7 to 10) are only needed if changes are testable from the front end or if this task is the final regression check.

7. [ ] `npm run compose:build:summary` - If status is `failed`, or item counts indicate failures/unknown in a failure run, inspect `logs/test-summaries/compose-build-latest.log` to find the failing target(s).

9. [ ] Manual Playwright-MCP testing step to manually confirm items within the story and general regression checks that should be detailed based on the task being tested. This should include a check to ensure that there are no logged errors within the debug console. The front end is accessible at http://host.docker.internal:5001 via the Playwright MCP tools.
Log review rule: only open full logs when a wrapper reports failure, unexpected warnings, or unknown/ambiguous failure counts. This preserves tokens while keeping full diagnostics available.
If possible, git push the branch.
